skill.name: youtube-search-api-skill
skill.description: Extract structured YouTube search results through the BrowserAct API. The skill runs a Python helper that starts a BrowserAct workflow, polls for completion, and prints results for videos, Shorts, channels, or playlists.
skill.summary: Search YouTube through BrowserAct and return structured result data.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The static critical heuristic is not confirmed as malicious. The skill intentionally sends a BrowserAct API key to the BrowserAct workflow API and asks the agent to run a local Python script, so it should be published with a clear network and credential-use warning.

medium_findings.title: BrowserAct API Key Sent in Authorization Header
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as a bearer token to the configured BrowserAct API endpoint. This is required for the skill, but users must understand that credential use and external network access occur.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and builds an Authorization header for requests to api.browseract.com. The behavior is explicit and matches the skill purpose, so the risk is credential exposure to the intended provider rather than confirmed theft.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Guided Script Execution with User Search Terms
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The instructions tell the agent to execute a Python script with user-controlled keywords, result type, and limit. This is expected behavior, but agents should pass arguments safely and avoid shell interpolation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is a normal invocation of the bundled script, but user-controlled keywords become command arguments. The risk depends on how the host agent invokes the command.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Most SKILL.md external command findings are inline examples, parameter names, supported values, or documented output fields. They do not execute code by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown documentation using backticks around strings and field names. Only the bash block at lines 47-50 represents an actual command recommendation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Signals Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged SKILL.md lines describe product claims, inputs, examples, and error handling. No hashing, encryption, system probing, or reconnaissance logic was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural language, parameter examples, output field names, and a task ID from an API response. No cryptographic or host reconnaissance operation is present.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The combination of network access, API-key handling, and script execution is part of the declared BrowserAct workflow. No evidence found of prompt injection, obfuscation, credential exfiltration to an unrelated endpoint, or hidden commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious combination is real but aligns with the disclosed purpose of calling BrowserAct. The assessment remains medium because the credential and network behavior still requires user trust.

dangerous_patterns.title: External API Calls with Bearer Token
dangerous_patterns.description: The script performs POST and GET requests to the BrowserAct workflow API using an Authorization bearer token. This is intended, but it is a sensitive network pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The requests and bearer header are explicit in the script. The endpoint is the declared BrowserAct API, which reduces maliciousness but not sensitivity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Invocation Recommended by Skill
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to run a local Python script. This is normal for this skill, but marketplaces should show that it requires script execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented command is direct evidence that the skill expects local execution. The command targets the bundled helper script rather than an arbitrary executable.

content.value_statement: YouTube search research often requires manual copying across videos, Shorts, channels, and playlists. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow and returns structured search result data for analysis.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python helper for the BrowserAct YouTube Search API workflow.
Accepts keywords, result type, and result limit as command-line inputs.
Supports Videos, Shorts, Channels, and Playlists result tabs.
Polls BrowserAct task status until completion, failure, cancellation, or timeout.
Prints returned result text or the available task response data.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY from BrowserAct.
Sends searches and the API key to BrowserAct services.
Depends on BrowserAct workflow availability and YouTube result behavior.
Does not open individual YouTube result pages for deeper extraction.

use_cases.title: Build Topic Result Lists
use_cases.description: Collect structured YouTube results for market, content, or audience research around a keyword.

use_cases.title: Track Competitor Visibility
use_cases.description: Search competitor names or product terms and compare visible videos, channels, and playlists.

use_cases.title: Find Creator Prospects
use_cases.description: Discover channels or Shorts related to a niche before outreach or partnership review.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with a simple keyword search.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube videos for "AI automation" and return up to 20 structured results.

prompt_templates.title: Shorts Topic Scan
prompt_templates.scenario: Focus on short-form content for a topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube Shorts for "productivity tools" with a limit of 50 results. Summarize common themes.

prompt_templates.title: Channel Discovery
prompt_templates.scenario: Identify creators in a specific niche.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube Channels for "home lab networking" and list promising channels with their visible metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Research Batch
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare several terms for market research.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run searches for "CRM tutorial", "sales automation", and "lead scoring". Compare result types and visible engagement signals.

output_examples.input: Search Videos for "AI automation" with a limit of 20.
output_examples.output: A structured list of visible YouTube video results with titles, snippets, view counts, publish timing, and URLs when available.

output_examples.input: Search Channels for "data engineering" with a limit of 30.
output_examples.output: A channel-focused result set that can support creator discovery, competitor mapping, or outreach planning.

output_examples.input: Search Playlists for "beginner Python" with a limit of 25.
output_examples.output: A playlist result summary that helps compare curated learning collections for the requested topic.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY only in a trusted environment before running the script.
Use clear keywords and choose the matching result type before starting a workflow.
Review returned results before using them for publication or business decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys into shared chats, logs, or documents.
Do not run broad searches without a clear limit or research purpose.
Do not treat visible search snippets as complete video or channel analysis.

faq.question: What API key does this skill require?
faq.answer: It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY from BrowserAct. The script reads it from the environment.

faq.question: Does it call YouTube directly?
faq.answer: No. The script calls the BrowserAct workflow API, which performs the YouTube search workflow.

faq.question: Which result types are supported?
faq.answer: The documented options are Videos, Shorts, Channels, and Playlists.

faq.question: Can it extract full video page details?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for search result data, not individual video page scraping.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: It uses external network requests and a BrowserAct API key. That behavior is intended but sensitive.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
